paper_title: Species Localities and Climate Data Analysis

methods.method_name: Species Locality Data Collection and Correction
methods.description: This method involves collecting and correcting species locality records from various sources to ensure accurate georeferencing for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: Bat-sampling locality records with decimal latitude and longitude coordinates were obtained from the Global Biodiversity Information Facility (GBIF; www.gbif.org) and from the literature.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch species locality records from GBIF and literature

steps.description: We plotted all points in ArcGIS v10.2 (ESRI, Redlands, CA) to assess the quality of the spatial data and we corrected localities that were clearly erroneously georeferenced (e.g., lying in the ocean).
steps.searchable_summary: plot and correct georeferenced locality data using ArcGIS

steps.description: Localities georeferenced from the literature were recorded at a resolution of 0.0001 decimal degrees, while points obtained from GBIF were kept at their original resolution.
Information on fossil localities was obtained from the literature and from collections held at Florida Museum of Natural History, and localities were subsequently georeferenced.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch and georeference fossil locality data

methods.description: This method involves collecting climate data, performing correlation analysis to select uncorrelated variables, and extracting climate information for species localities.

steps.description: We used climate data from 19 WorldClim variables at 30 sec (ca. 1 km 2 ) spatial resolution for current, mid-Holocene (HOL, ca. 6 ka), and Last Glacial Maximum (LGM, ca. 21 ka) climate (Hijmans et al., 2005).
steps.searchable_summary: fetch WorldClim climate data for different time periods

steps.description: We performed a Pearson correlation analysis in ENMTools v1.4.3 (Warren et al., 2010) on the 19 climate variables to avoid issues with collinearity and chose seven uncorrelated variables to represent regional temperature and precipitation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Pearson correlation analysis to select uncorrelated climate variables

steps.description: We extracted climate information for our species localities using ArcGIS v10.2.
steps.searchable_summary: extract climate data for species localities using ArcGIS

steps.description: To correct for this, we ran models by randomly sampling 3000 background localities within the Caribbean region.
steps.searchable_summary: sample background localities for climate modeling

steps.description: We used a 2 km buffer around species locality points and removed background points located within this buffer to prevent bias in our background climate dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: apply buffer to remove biased background points
